title: chaetocarpus humilis
intro: Chaetocarpus humilis, also known as the 'dwarf ironplow', is a plant species that belongs to the family Clusiaceae. It is primarily found in tropical regions, particularly in West Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Chaetocarpus humilis can be prepared as a herbal remedy by infusing its leaves in water, which is then used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
